records.ety: [L. redundans, -antis, p. pr. of redundare: cf. F. redondant. See Redound.]

defs: Exceeding what is natural or necessary; superabundant; exuberant; as, a redundant quantity of bile or food.
quotes: Notwithstanding the redundant oil in fishes, they do not increase fat so much as flesh.

defs: Using more worrds or images than are necessary or useful; pleonastic.
quotes: Where an suthor is redundant, mark those paragraphs to be retrenched.
